title: Crewship Routine DSL
description: Workspace-scoped declarative AI workflow recipe. Same shape the server's pipeline.Parse + pipeline.Validate functions accept. Add `"$schema": "./schemas/routine.v1.json"` (or the published URL) to a routine.json to get inline editor validation + autocomplete in VSCode, JetBrains, etc.

dsl_version.description: DSL schema version. Forward-compat field; server rejects unknown values.

name.description: Workspace-unique slug. 1-64 chars, lowercase alpha-num + hyphen/underscore, must start with alpha-num (matches server slugRE).

display_name.description: Human-readable label rendered in lists. Optional; falls back to `name` when absent.

description.description: One-line summary shown in the [AVAILABLE ROUTINES] system-prompt block + UI lists.

inputs.description: Declared input parameters. Each input is template-substitutable as {{ inputs.<name> }}.

outputs.description: Declared output names. Read from the final step's output by name. MVP: documentary + UI rendering only.

execution_tier.description: Optional override of workspace tier mapping. Per-step `complexity` still wins; this sets the default.

estimated_cost_usd.description: Author estimate. UI surfaces cost overruns; not enforced.

max_cost_usd.description: Runtime cost cap. Run aborts with FAILED status if cumulative cost exceeds this between steps.

concurrency_key.description: Template-substituted partition key. Same workspace + same rendered key gates runs to MaxConcurrent at a time. Empty = unlimited parallelism.

max_concurrent.description: Cap on simultaneous runs for the resolved concurrency_key. Defaults to 1 when concurrency_key is set, ignored otherwise.

egress_targets.description: Declared outbound hostnames. Enforced for `http` step type via the workspace egress allowlist.

credentials_required.description: Declared credential needs. Type-matched, not ID-matched, so the routine ports across workspaces.

steps.description: Ordered (or DAG-structured via needs[]) sequence of steps. At least one step required.

agentless.description: Token-zero guarantee: the routine may only contain http/code/wait/transform/notify steps and can never invoke an LLM. See Agentless routines in docs/guides/routines.

parallelism.description: How independent steps are scheduled. 'explicit' (default, and when omitted): parallel DAG only where a step declares needs[]; otherwise steps run in source order. 'auto': independence is derived from data flow — a step that references no prior step's {{ steps.X }} output runs in the first wave, so sibling steps fan out concurrently (bounded) without hand-wiring needs[]. 'off': force sequential source-order execution even if needs[] are declared. Opt-in: 'auto' changes the implicit depends-on-previous semantics, so existing routines are unaffected unless they set it.

integrations_required.description: Third-party integrations (Composio) the author crew must have connected before the routine runs. Lowercased/trimmed; gated at run time.

resources.description: Agent-declared capability manifest — datastores/tools the routine's container needs. Gated at run time. See docs/guides/routines 'resources block'.

guardrails.description: Per-routine input/output safety-scanning configuration. See docs/guides/routines.

eval.description: Continuous grading of production runs (online sampler + offline regression suite). See docs/guides/routines 'Observability'.

RoutineHooks.description: Routine-level lifecycle steps run around the main step graph. Each is a Step restricted to deterministic side-channel types (code | http | transform) — no agent_run (a hook must not recurse or spend tokens). before_all aborts the run on failure; after_all runs after a COMPLETED run; on_failure after a FAILED run.

name.description: Identifier used in {{ inputs.<name> }} substitution. C-style: starts with letter or _.

default.description: Default value if input is omitted at invocation. Type must match `type`.

min.description: Minimum (numeric types only). *float64 for decimal support.

type.description: Credential type the runtime resolves at run time (e.g. 'anthropic', 'github', 'stripe').

id.description: Unique within this routine. Referenced via {{ steps.<id>.output }}.

model_override.description: Explicit (adapter, model) pin that wins over `complexity` resolution.

timeout_seconds.description: Step-level timeout. Tighter of (agent default, this) wins.

if.description: Optional gate. A bare CEL expression is evaluated against inputs / steps / run (e.g. `inputs.dry_run == false`, `steps.classify == "spam"`); a {{ ... }} template is rendered then truthy-checked (back-compat). Falsey (empty/false/0/null/nil/no/off) → step skipped.

needs.description: DAG dependency. Independent steps with disjoint needs[] execute in parallel.

on_fail.description: Behavior when step's validation gate fails.

inputs.description: Used by call_pipeline. Inputs forwarded to the nested routine.

ForeachStep.description: Fan a body of steps out over each element of a JSON array with bounded concurrency, collecting per-item outputs into a JSON array.

items.description: Template rendering to a JSON array, e.g. {{ inputs.urls }} or {{ steps.fetch.output }}.

as.description: Name the loop variable is bound to in each body step's context, read as {{ inputs.<as> }}.

parallelism.description: Max concurrent items. 0 = default wave concurrency; 1 = sequential. Clamped to the wave cap.

steps.description: Per-item body. Runs sequentially within an item, in parallel across items. wait / call_pipeline / nested foreach are not allowed.

Outcomes.description: Rubric-based output evaluation by a separate grader agent. Runs after structural Validation passes. Iterates the worker if rubric fails (up to max_iterations). agent_run-only.

grader_agent_slug.description: Slug of an agent in the routine's AUTHOR crew that grades worker output. Cross-family judging recommended (smart-tier judge for fast-tier worker).

max_iterations.description: Cap on the worker→grade→revise→grade loop. 1 = single shot, no revision. Default 3.

on_fail.description: Behaviour when the rubric ultimately can't be satisfied after max_iterations is exhausted.

name.description: Stable identifier the grader returns in its verdict. Unique within criteria[].

rule.description: Natural-language pass/fail statement the grader evaluates against.

description.description: Optional longer-form context for the grader.

Validation.description: Step output gate. Schema is JSON Schema draft 2020-12 subset; must_not_contain / must_contain / min_length / max_length are Crewship extensions.

schema.description: JSON Schema draft 2020-12 subset: type, required, properties, items, pattern, format, enum, minLength, maxLength, minimum, maximum, etc.

must_not_contain.description: Output must include none of these substrings. Credential-leak tripwire.

must_contain.description: Output must include all of these substrings.

url.description: Template-substitutable. Host enforced against egress allowlist + redirect targets.

headers.description: Header values are template-substitutable; KEYS are NOT (security: prevents Authorization overwrite).

runtime.description: Sandboxed, deterministic, token-zero runners wired in this build. 'python' | 'go' | 'bash' are NOT wired and are rejected at author time — use expr/cel for agentless logic, or an agent_run step. See docs/manifest/routine.md 'Code steps'.

code.description: Script body. Template-substitutable. Inputs auto-mapped to CREWSHIP_INPUT_<NAME> env vars.

ScriptStep.description: Execs a BUNDLED script that lives in the crew shared dir (delivered with the routine / via `crewship crew files save`) inside the crew container. Deterministic, token-zero — the first-class replacement for 'agent_run whose prompt tells the LLM to shell out'. Stdout is the step output; exit != 0 fails the step.

path.description: Path to the script under /crew/shared (e.g. 'scripts/parse_vypis.py'). Traversal + absolute paths escaping /crew/shared are rejected.

interpreter.description: Interpreter to run the script with (e.g. python3, bash, node). Inferred from the extension (.py→python3, .sh→bash, .js→node, …) when omitted.

args.description: Arguments passed to the script (template-substitutable). Passed as argv — never through a shell, so they cannot inject.

env.description: Additional env vars (template-substitutable values). Declared inputs also flow as CREWSHIP_INPUT_<NAME>.

timeout_sec.description: Hard timeout. 0 = no timeout (wait forever; not recommended).

NotifyStep.description: Non-blocking inbox message pushed mid-run. Renders title/body from the run context, scrubs secrets, and posts to a recipient's inbox, then continues. At least one of title/body is required.

to.description: Recipient selector: 'workspace' (default), 'trigger' (the user who ran it; workspace fallback if unattended), 'user:<id>', 'role:OWNER', 'role:MANAGER', or 'crew:<slug>' (fans out to every human member of that crew in this workspace; an unknown slug or an empty crew degrades to a workspace notice). Template-substitutable.

RetryPolicy.description: Per-step retry policy for transient EXECUTION failures (HTTP 5xx, timeouts, rate limits, net blips). Distinct from on_fail, which handles a validation/rubric failure. The loop is cost-aware: it stops mid-retry rather than breaching max_cost_usd.

max_attempts.description: Total attempts including the first. Capped at 10.

retry_on.description: CEL predicate deciding whether an error is retryable. Sees `error` (message string), `transient` (bool: the built-in 429/5xx/timeout classifier), and `status` (int: the HTTP status an http step failed with, parsed from its 'HTTP <code>' error; 0 for any other error). Must evaluate to bool. Empty = retry any error. E.g. 'transient' or 'status == 429' or 'status >= 500' or 'error.contains("429")'.

BackoffPolicy.description: Delay growth between retry attempts. First retry waits min_ms; each subsequent delay is multiplied by factor and clamped at max_ms.

jitter.description: Full jitter — actual sleep is uniform in [0, delay). Default true.

StepHooks.description: Per-step lifecycle steps: before runs ahead of this step (its failure fails the step); after runs once the step completes (best-effort). Restricted to code | http | transform.
